title: Richly Spiced Thai Duck Legs Curry
description: A mustardy and light central Thai dish featuring duck legs, this curry is perfect for adventurous eaters, and is low sodium friendly.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and blanch the duck legs for 5 minutes to remove excess fat.
Remove the duck legs from the water and set them aside to cool.
In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat and add the mustard seeds, letting them pop for 1 minute.
Add the red curry paste to the pan and stir for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk, fish sauce, brown sugar, and water to the pan, stirring to combine.
Add the blanched duck legs to the curry sauce and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the curry simmer for 20 minutes, or until the duck is tender.
Stir in the chopped basil and serve the curry hot over rice.
